notes: v1.7.6 — FRONT-DOOR REBUILD: the WebGL globe landing. Replaces the app's boot landing (the old <div id=boot> header/topology/Enter-circle + drift-stars canvas) with the v7 globe: real-Earth point cloud (SPECTRUM palette by longitude), drag-to-rotate w/ inertia, two stats (GLOBAL STATUS·ONLINE / OFFLINE CACHE·100%), a 4-row status card, and a reactor enter-ring (HOLD ~0.9s to charge → 1.7s warp-to-Austin → flash → app handoff). TRANSPLANTED VERBATIM from phantom-landing-v7.html (the visual contract), via a recon→prefix-transform→harden→review pipeline. NAMESPACE ISOLATION (the #1 risk): the reference used generic names; ALL 44 classes / 10 ids / 3 keyframes were prefixed `pl-` (CSS+HTML+JS in lockstep) by a transform script. Beyond the handoff's list, recon caught TWO more clobber vectors the spec missed: the reference's `:root` redefines --cyan/--violet/--green/--bg (which the app uses everywhere) and it has global `*`/`html,body` resets — these were DROPPED; the landing palette is re-scoped as `--pl-*` vars on #boot, and a `#boot,#boot *{box-sizing}` scope added. Only `.card` + `.stat` actually collided; verified zero pl- leakage outside the landing. WIRING: finishWarp() now calls the app's existing launch() (the flash frame masks the transition; launch collapses #boot over the revealed app) with a double-fire guard; the demo `.done` overlay + `.note` block + Google-Fonts <link> were removed; the wordmark repointed from Audiowide → the app's embedded @font-face 'PhantomBrand' (offline). HARDENING (required, added — not in the mockup): no-WebGL fallback (static chrome + tap-the-ring → launch, app stays enterable with zero GL); webglcontextlost(preventDefault+stop)/webglcontextrestored(reload re-init) handlers (iOS memory-pressure freeze guard); prefers-reduced-motion (CSS kills arc-spin/dot/core animations + JS kills globe auto-spin); the reference's visibilitychange stop/resume already present. The removed boot canvas is referenced by the app's drift-stars IIFE but it is already null-guarded (if(!canvas)return) — clean no-op, no crash. DATA: card metas de-fictioned (dropped fabricated '633 cabs·19,302 hosts' / '132 cards' / '18,432 refs' — no fiction on the front door); version eyebrow JS-bound to PHANTOM_APP_VERSION (dropped hardcoded 'Coreweave Austin · V1.7.4', de-bake-friendly). SIZE: ~7.9KB CSS + ~3.9KB HTML + ~73KB JS (mostly the engine + Earth point data) added to dct-ios.html; one rAF loop, stops on hidden + after handoff, zero leaked globals (full IIFE), no new cache entries, no network deps. VERIFIED: node --check 3/3 inline scripts + sw.js OK, JSON valid, block-0 braces 2566 -> 2636 (+70, the landing CSS + boot-items :empty rule; new baseline 2636), balanced; three stamps v1.7.6. Built via recon-workflow + adversarial-review-workflow on c7c54b3-derived tree (post v1.7.5). ⚠️ NOT YET DEVICE-VERIFIED — the handoff GATES THE SHIP on an iPhone-Safari pass list (cold load / offline load / drag-rotate w/ scroll / hold-charge-cancel / warp→handoff / bg-fg GL freeze / zero CSS bleed / battery). This is the highest-risk surface (if it breaks, the app won't open). DO NOT trust as production until that pass runs.
